data.title: Western Star Resources inks deal to acquire The Real American Corp.
VSee Health granted Nasdaq extension to regain compliance
data.description:
• VSee Health (NASDAQ:VSEE) received approval from Nasdaq.
  • The extension requires VSEE to file delayed Q1 and Q2 2025 Form 10-Q reports by October 15, 2025.
  • The company must also demonstrate compliance with Nasdaq’s equity rule by December 1, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Acadia Healthcare gains as activist Engine Capital reports 3% stake
data.description:
Acadia Healthcare (NASDAQ:ACHC) rose 4% in premarket trading after activist Engine Capital reported a 3% stake in the behavioral healthcare operator and is pushing for board changes and other moves. 

The activist investor is urging Acadia Healthcare (NASDAQ:ACHC) to halt capital-intensive growth projects, fix its core business, and review its asset portfolio, according to a letter  Engine Capital wrote to the company's board on Wednesday. 

"We believe the Company’s undervaluation and sustained negative returns are due to ineffective execution, a bloated corporate structure, poor decision-making, a revolving management team under CEO Christopher Hunter, a lack of behavioral health experience among executives and directors, a poorly designed compensation framework, and a culture that has emphasized “growth at any cost,”'  Engine Capital Managing Partner Arnaud Ajdler wrote in the letter. 

Acadia Healthcare didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. News of the activist stake was earlier reported by the Financial Times. 

Shares of Acadia Healthcare have fallen 71% over the past year.

data.title: Schwab wants to unlock more private-firm stakes for retail investors, CEO says - report
data.description:
Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) CEO Officer Rick Wurster said Wednesday that the brokerage giant is seeking to let everyday investors buy into private companies, pointing to fintech Stripe (STRIP) and artificial-intelligence firm OpenAI as companies that retail investors should be able to invest in.

In an interview with Bloomberg Television, Wurster said “there’s a lot more private companies today than there used to be, and fewer public companies, and so I do think it makes sense to think about providing direct access to retail investors to private companies, and that’s something we’re looking at and thinking about how we participate in."

His remarks come after Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) in April launched a platform providing access to alternative investments like private equity and real estate for eligible retail clients with over $5M in household assets.

The broader access to private firms that Schwab (SCHW) is mulling offering would deepen its competition with the likes of Robinhood Markets (HOOD), which earlier in the month filed plans for a new closed-end fund that would give everyday investors exposure to privately held companies.

“We would love to see retail investors be able to participate in the growth of private companies in our country,” Wurster told Bloomberg.

data.title: Citigroup to redeem $2.75B in 5.610% notes due 2026
data.description:
• Citigroup (NYSE:C) will redeem all $2.75B of its 5.610% fixed rate/floating rate notes due 2026.
  • The redemption will occur on September 29, 2025, at par plus accrued and unpaid interest.
  • Interest on the notes will cease to accrue starting on the redemption date.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Ballston Spa Bancorp and NBC Bancorp announce $1.3B merger of equals
data.description:
Ballston Spa Bancorp (OTCPK:BSPA) and NBC Bancorp (OTCPK:NCXS) announced a strategic merger to form a $1.3B community bank.

The deal is valued at approximately $26M based on BSNB’s current share price of $68.21. BSNB plans to raise $20M in subordinated debt before closing to fund growth. 

NBC shareholders will receive 0.8065 BSNB shares per NBC share in a tax-free stock-for-stock transaction. BSNB shareholders will own 66% of the combined company, with NBC shareholders owning 34%.

The combined bank will operate under BSNB’s charter, with NBC branches branded as “Coxsackie Bank, a division of Ballston Spa National Bank.”

BSNB chief excutive officer Christopher R. Dowd will become CEO of the combined bank; NBC CEO John A. Balli will serve as President. The merged entity’s board will include 9 directors from BSNB and 4 from NBC.

The merger is expected to close in Q2 2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals. The combined company will retain the Ballston Spa Bancorp, Inc. name and trade under the BSPA ticker.

The merger enhances scale, lending capacity, and regional presence while maintaining local decision-making. 

Source: Press release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: PYPL, AMZN, PFGC, CRCL
data.description:
Stock index futures edged up Wednesday, as Micron’s (MU) strong forecast lifted Nasdaq futures, a day after Wall Street’s three-day record-setting streak stalled. Here are four stocks to keep an eye on for the day: 

PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) shares rose 1.31% in premarket trade as the firm and Blue Owl Capital (NYSE:OWL) said on Wednesday that funds managed by the alternative asset manager will acquire ~$7B worth of PayPal's buy now, pay later receivables. The two companies have executed a two-year agreement.

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) stock was up 1.5% before the opening bell as Wells Fargo boosted its rating on the company to Overweight from Equal Weight, adding it has greater conviction in AWS revenue acceleration in 2026, supported by Project Rainier capacity additions and peak share losses in 2025. Analyst Ken Gawrelski does expect Project Rainier to cut into profits.

Performance Food Group (NYSE:PFGC) will be keenly watched as the firm disclosed that it entered a cooperation agreement with activist investor Sachem Head Capital Management LP. The terms of the cooperation agreement include the board increasing to thirteen directors, with the appointment of Sachem Head Scott Ferguson.

Circle’s (NYSE:CRCL) shares were up 2.3% in premarket trade as its closest rival, Tether, confirmed capital raise plans at a reported $500B valuation. The stablecoin Tether (USDT-USD), pegged to the U.S. dollar, recently topped a market cap of $172 billion. Its closest rival, Circle’s (NYSE:CRCL) USDC (USDC-USD), holds a market value of about $74 billion.

data.title: Valley National raised to Buy at Jefferies on visible path for 15% ROTCE
data.description:
Jefferies upgraded Valley National Bancorp (NASDAQ:VLY) to Buy from Hold on Wednesday as analyst David Chiaverini sees a clear path for the bank to achieve its 15% return on tangible common equity by mid-2028.

Valley National (NASDAQ:VLY) stock rose 2.2% in premarket trading.

Chiaverini cites the bank's net interest margin gains, better credit costs, tight expense control, and stronger loan growth for his optimism. As a result, a 15% ROTCE "should also help close VLY's 15% valuation gap to peers, in our view," he wrote in a note to clients.

"Valley's NIM has expanded five consecutive quarters through 2Q'25, and we think this positive trend should continue through 2025 and into 2026," the analyst said. "We expect expansion to be driven by ongoing fixed asset and deposit repricing tailwinds."

He also sees stabilizing commercial real estate balances and continued strength in commercial & industrial supporting an uptick in loan growth. 

Jefferies' Buy rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating and average SA Analyst rating, both at Hold, and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Meet Gatik: One of the biggest disruptors in the autonomous commercial trucking sector
data.description:
Autonomous trucking startup firm Gatik continues to emerge as one of the leading companies in the self-driving truck space.

The Mountain View, California-based company announced a significant expansion of its commercial partnership with Loblaw Companies Limited (OTCPK:LBLCF) in a development that it said marks a milestone for the autonomous trucking industry.

Loblaw (OTCPK:LBLCF) will deploy Gatik's autonomous fleet across its distribution network in the Greater Toronto Area. Under the initial phase of the expansion, 20 autonomous trucks equipped with Gatik's next-generation sensor suite will be deployed by the end of 2025, with a further 30 autonomous trucks to be deployed by the end of 2026, creating a scalable platform for future growth phases. The autonomous commercial truck fleet could be one of the largest in North America.

The expanded fleet of Isuzu-built trucks will initially be deployed with safety drivers onboard before transitioning to freight only (driverless) operations across Loblaw's (OTCPK:LBLCF) regional distribution networks, delivering goods with greater frequency and responsiveness to over 300 Loblaw stores.

Under the expanded partnership, Loblaw (LBLF) also made a strategic investment in Gatik.

Notably, Walmart (WMT), Kroger (KR), and Tyson Foods (TSN) also leverage Gatik trucks for regional and urban distribution, including driver-out, daily commercial deliveries.

Gatik was established in 2017 by Gautam Narang, Arjun Narang, and Apeksha Kumavat, with operations in Arkansas, Texas, and Canada. Initially operating in stealth mode, Gatik developed its Level 4 autonomous trucking technology before launching pilots with commercial partners.

Some of the most significant recent backers are Nippon Express, Isuzu (OTCPK:ISUZY) (OTCPK:ISUZF), ITOCHU Corporation, Koch Disruptive Technologies, Ryder (R), Goodyear (GT), FM Capital, Innovation Endeavors, Dynamo Ventures, Wittington Ventures, Trucks VC, Intact Ventures, Alumni Ventures, and ARK Venture Fund (ARKVX).

Other commercial autonomous trucking players for Class 5-8 vehicles include Aurora Innovation (AUR), Kodiak Robotics, Waymo (GOOG), TuSimple, Plus, Torc (OTCPK:DTRUY), Volvo Autonomous Solutions, and Embark Truck.

data.title: Healthmine appoints Dwight Erskine as CEO
data.description:
• Healthmine (NASDAQ:HCAT) on Wednesday announced that its Board of Directors has appointed Dwight Erskine as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective September 15, 2025.

  • Erskine succeeds Bryce Williams, who will remain with the company during a transition period before moving into an advisory role, said the company. 

  • Erskine most recently served as senior vice president of Enterprise Transformation at VillageMD. Prior to that role, he was the healthcare leader at Synergetics.

  • Press release
